title: biscutella intermedia var. subscaposa
intro: Biscutella intermedia var. subscaposa is a rare subspecies of the Biscutella intermedia plant, a member of the Brassicaceae family. It is a flowering plant that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe and is also known as the "alpine biscutella." The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Biscutella intermedia var. subscaposa is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues, and is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water.
